target.title: fix(ollama): keep OpenAI-compatible tool_call arguments as strings
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96441. When using Ollama Cloud models via the OpenAI-compatible endpoint (e.g. `ollama/glm-5.2:cloud`), the second agent turn fails with:

```
400 json: cannot unmarshal object into Go struct field .messages.tool_calls.function.arguments of type string
```

OpenClaw converts assistant `tool_calls.function.arguments` back from a JSON string into an object when injecting the `num_ctx` option. The OpenAI API spec requires `arguments` to be a string, and Ollama Cloud's Go server enforces that strictly.

## Why This Change Was Made

The generic OpenAI-completions formatter already stringifies `tool_calls.function.arguments`. The Ollama-compat `num_ctx` wrapper was the only place that re-parsed those strings into objects, which regressed in 2026.6.9. Removing that normalization restores spec-compliant behavior for OpenAI-compatible Ollama endpoints while leaving the native `/api/chat` path untouched (it still objectifies arguments as required by Ollama's native format).

## User Impact

Users of Ollama Cloud models can now have multi-turn conversations that include tool calls without hitting a 400 error on the second turn.

## Evidence

- Added a regression test in `extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts` that passes an assistant message with stringified `tool_calls.function.arguments` through `createConfiguredOllamaCompatStreamWrapper` and asserts the arguments stay a string.
- Ran the targeted test before the fix: it failed because arguments were parsed to an object.
- Ran the targeted test after the fix: it passes.
- Ran the full Ollama extension test suite (`extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts` and `extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts`): 117 tests pass.
- Ran `oxlint` on the changed files: 0 warnings, 0 errors.
- Ran `oxfmt` on the changed files.
target.author: moguangyu5-design
